2606.23196.title: When Does Intrinsic Self-Correction Help? A Task-Sensitive Analysis
2606.23196.paper_summary: Intrinsic self-correction (SC) aims to improve large language model outputs by prompting a model to revisit its own initial answer without external feedback. Recent studies have questioned the reliability of this approach, showing that models often struggle to judge whether their initial responses are correct. In this work, we take a task-sensitive view of SC. Rather than asking whether it works in general, we examine settings where SC may operate through different mechanisms: verifying explicit constraints, revisiting a complex reasoning process, or providing a second opinion over competing strategies in word-game tasks. Across multiple benchmarks and models, we find that SC can yield consistent performance gains when the underlying task structure facilitates these modes of revision. These results suggest that SC is best understood as a task-dependent inference-time strategy whose usefulness depends on the role the revision stage can play in a given task, rather than as a uniformly reliable method for improving initial model outputs.
2606.23196.paper_summary_zh: 內在自我修正 (SC) 旨在通過促使模型重新檢視其初始答案而無需外部反饋來改善大型語言模型的輸出。最近的研究質疑了這種方法的可靠性，顯示模型通常難以判斷其初始回應是否正確。在這項工作中，我們採取了對 SC 的任務敏感視角。我們並不是在問這種方法是否普遍有效，而是檢視 SC 可能通過不同機制運作的情境：驗證明確的約束、重新檢視複雜的推理過程，或在字詞遊戲任務中提供對競爭策略的第二意見。在多個基準和模型中，我們發現當基礎任務結構促進這些修訂模式時，SC 可以帶來穩定的性能提升。這些結果表明，SC 最好被理解為一種依賴於任務的推理時間策略，其有效性取決於修訂階段在特定任務中所能扮演的角色，而不是作為一種普遍可靠的方法來改善初始模型輸出。
2606.23196.author: Elroy Stav et.al.
2606.23196.authors: Elroy Stav, Dvir Berlowitz, Maayan Orner, Sarit Kraus
